truth.papers.A65.title: Melting, Processing, and Properties of Disordered Fe-Al and Fe-Al-C Based Alloys
truth.papers.A65.keywords[2]: air induction melting with flux cover
truth.papers.A65.paper_scope: To develop cost-effective melting and processing routes (particularly air induction melting with flux cover, AIMFC) for Fe-Al and Fe-Al-C lightweight steels, evaluate microstructure and mechanical properties, and study the effect of carbon on Fe-7Al alloys.

prediction.papers.A65.title: Melting, Processing, and Properties of Disordered Fe-Al and Fe-Al-C Based Alloys
prediction.papers.A65.paper_scope: Development of cost-effective melting and processing routes for disordered Fe-7Al and Fe-9Al lightweight steels, evaluation of microstructure and mechanical properties, and study of carbon addition (0.002–1.0 wt.%) on Fe-7Al alloy.

truth.alloys.alloy_fe7al.alloys_notes: Minor impurities: Si, Mn, S, O, N. Represents the base Fe-7Al alloy used in AIM, AIMFC, VIM processes.
truth.alloys.alloy_fe9al.alloys_notes: Expected to exhibit short-range ordering. Minor impurities similar to Fe-7Al.
truth.alloys.alloy_7al_002c.alloys_notes: Actual composition: Al 6.86, C 0.002 wt.% (Table IV).

prediction.alloys.alloy_fe7al.alloys_notes: Measured AIMFC composition: Al 7.30, Si 0.14, Mn 0.045, S 0.0016, C 0.019, O 0.0057, H 0.0023, N 0.0018 wt.%. Measured VIM composition: Al 7.02, Si 0.12, Mn 0.052, S 0.0028, C 0.015, O 0.0035, H 0.0020, N <0.001 wt.%.
prediction.alloys.alloy_fe9al.alloys_notes: Measured AIMFC composition: Al 9.20, Si 0.16, Mn 0.046, S 0.0015, C 0.017, O 0.0050, H 0.0052, N 0.0015 wt.%. Measured VIM composition: Al 9.10, Si 0.11, Mn 0.042, S 0.0027, C 0.014, O 0.0023, H 0.0021, N <0.001 wt.%.
prediction.alloys.alloy_7al_002c.alloys_notes: Measured composition: Al 6.86, Si 0.18, Mn 0.16, S 0.004, C 0.002 wt.%.
prediction.alloys.alloy_7al_015c.alloys_notes: Measured composition: Al 6.92, Si 0.17, Mn 0.18, S 0.005, C 0.15 wt.%.
prediction.alloys.alloy_7al_035c.alloys_notes: Measured composition: Al 6.78, Si 0.19, Mn 0.15, S 0.004, C 0.35 wt.%.
prediction.alloys.alloy_7al_05c.alloys_notes: Measured composition: Al 6.95, Si 0.15, Mn 0.12, S 0.004, C 0.50 wt.%.
prediction.alloys.alloy_7al_10c.alloys_notes: Measured composition: Al 7.10, Si 0.18, Mn 0.13, S 0.002, C 1.00 wt.%.

truth.processes.proc_7al_aim.description: Air induction melting (AIM) without flux cover, followed by preheating (1373 K, 1 h), hot forging, preheating (1373 K, 1 h) and hot rolling to 12 mm with 50% reduction, reheating after every six passes.
truth.processes.proc_7al_aim.processes_notes: AIM ingot sound and free from microcracks for Fe-7Al. Fe-9Al showed gas porosity and was not hot-worked.
truth.processes.proc_7al_aimfc.description: Air induction melting with flux cover (AIMFC) using protective slag (Al2O3, CaO, MgO, CaF2), melt held at 1900 K for 2 min, cast into split iron molds; subsequent hot forging and hot rolling identical to AIM procedure.
truth.processes.proc_7al_aimfc.processes_notes: Flux cover prevents hydrogen pick-up; excellent aluminum recovery (95-98%); low sulfur due to desulfurization by CaO and MgO in slag. Hydrogen content higher than VIM but lower than AIM.
truth.processes.proc_7al_vim.description: Vacuum induction melting (VIM) at 10^-4 torr, melt temperature 1893 K, cast in 80 mm split cast iron molds inside vacuum chamber; subsequently hot forged and hot rolled as in AIM.
truth.processes.proc_7al_vim.processes_notes: Alumina crucible; soft iron then aluminum charged; very low hydrogen (<1 ppm expected), sound porosity-free ingots.
truth.processes.proc_9al_aimfc.description: AIMFC process (similar to proc_7al_aimfc) applied to Fe-9Al alloy, followed by preheating (1373 K, 1 h), hot forging, and hot rolling.
truth.processes.proc_9al_aimfc.processes_notes: Ingots free from gas porosity but exhibited microcracks attributed to hydrogen embrittlement; hot workability poor.
truth.processes.proc_9al_vim.description: VIM process (similar to proc_7al_vim) for Fe-9Al alloy, followed by hot forging and hot rolling.
truth.processes.proc_9al_vim.processes_notes: Sound ingots, no microcracks; good hot workability.
truth.processes.proc_7al_aimfc_cr.description: AIMFC melting, hot forging, hot rolling, then cold rolling to 2 mm thickness and annealing.
truth.processes.proc_7al_aimfc_cr.processes_no: Cold rolling parameters and annealing details not provided; yields sheet/foil with excellent room-temperature ductility.

prediction.processes.proc_7al_aim.description: Air induction melting (AIM) without flux cover, followed by hot forging and hot rolling to 12 mm plate.
prediction.processes.proc_7al_aim.processes_notes: As-cast ingots exhibited gas porosity and higher hydrogen, oxygen, and sulfur levels.
prediction.processes.proc_7al_aimfc.description: Air induction melting with proprietary flux cover (Al2O3-CaO-MgO-CaF2) and charging schedule, followed by hot forging and hot rolling to 12 mm plate.
prediction.processes.proc_7al_aimfc.processes_notes: Produced sound, porosity-free ingots with low sulfur and oxygen comparable to VIM.
prediction.processes.proc_7al_vim.description: Vacuum induction melting (VIM) at 10^-4 torr with alumina-lined crucible, poured at 1893 K, followed by hot forging and hot rolling to 12 mm plate.
prediction.processes.proc_7al_vim.processes_notes: Very low hydrogen and impurity levels; excellent Al recovery (96–98%).
prediction.processes.proc_9al_aimfc.description: Air induction melting with flux cover for Fe-9Al, followed by hot forging and hot rolling to 12 mm plate.
prediction.processes.proc_9al_aimfc.processes_notes: Ingots free from gas porosity but exhibited microcracks due to higher residual hydrogen; poor hot workability.
prediction.processes.proc_9al_vim.description: Vacuum induction melting for Fe-9Al, followed by hot forging and hot rolling to 12 mm plate.
prediction.processes.proc_9al_vim.processes_notes: Sound ingots with excellent hot workability and low hydrogen.
prediction.processes.proc_fe7al_c_aimfc_hr.descript: Air induction melting with flux cover for Fe-7Al-C alloys with varying carbon (0.002–1.0 wt.%), followed by hot forging and hot rolling to 12 mm plate.
prediction.processes.proc_fe7al_c_aimfc_hr.processe: Used to study effect of carbon on microstructure and mechanical properties.
prediction.processes.proc_7al_aimfc_cr.description: Air induction melting with flux cover for Fe-7Al, followed by hot forging, hot rolling, cold rolling, and annealing to 2 mm sheet.
prediction.processes.proc_7al_aimfc_cr.processes_no: Cold-rolled and annealed sheet exhibited excellent room-temperature ductility (about 40% elongation).

truth.characterization_methods.idx_0.purpose: Examine grain structure and precipitates in hot-rolled condition
truth.characterization_methods.idx_0.key_findi: Coarse equiaxed grains in all alloys; Fe3AlC0.5 precipitates observed in carbon-containing alloys, volume fraction increasing with C content.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_7al_015c: Fe3Al peaks in low-C alloy; Fe3Al and Fe3AlC0.5 peaks present in alloys with 0.15-1.0 wt.% C.

measure hydrogen content in as-cast ingots
Hydrogen levels: AIM Fe-7Al 0.0052 wt.%, AIMFC Fe-7Al 0.0023 wt.%, VIM Fe-7Al 0.0020 wt.%, AIMFC Fe-9Al 0.0052 wt.%, VIM Fe-9Al 0.0021 wt.%.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_7al_aimf: Annealed microstructure with coarse equiaxed grains; grain size ~195 μm.
Annealed microstructure with coarse equiaxed grains; grain size ~186 μm.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_9al_aimf: Annealed microstructure with coarse equiaxed grains; grain size ~228 μm; microcracks present.

prediction.characterization_methods.sample_7al_002c: microstructural examination of carbon-containing Fe-7Al alloys
Single-phase structure with no precipitates in 0.002C alloy; precipitates appear at higher carbon levels.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_7al_015c: phase identification of precipitates in carbon-containing Fe-7Al alloys
Fe3AlC0.5 precipitates confirmed by XRD using Cu-Ka radiation on Philips 3710 diffractometer; similar peaks observed in 0.35–1.0 wt.% C alloys.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Fe-Al alloys containing 6–9 wt.% aluminum exhibit a density reduction of approximately 10% compared with conventional steels, offering high specific elastic stiffness and strength.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Fe-9Al alloy ingots produced by AIM exhibited extensive gas porosity and could not be thermomechanically processed or characterized for mechanical properties.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Aluminum recovery during AIMFC and VIM is 95–98%, which is superior to AIM and comparable between AIMFC and VIM.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: AIMFC ingots exhibit lower sulfur content than AIM and VIM ingots due to desulfurization by CaO present in the flux cover via slag–metal reactions.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Table V caption and Fig. 5 caption refer to the carbon-series alloys as 'AIM', whereas the main text explicitly states these alloys were prepared by air induction melting with a flux cover (AIMFC).
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Fe-9Al alloy is expected to exhibit short-range ordering, whereas Fe-7Al is a totally disordered solid solution.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: Cold-rolled and annealed Fe-7Al AIMFC sheet (2 mm thickness) exhibited excellent room-temperature ductility of about 40%, compared with 18% for the hot-rolled AIMFC condition.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: The poor room-temperature ductility of Fe-7Al produced by AIM is attributed to hydrogen embrittlement and higher oxygen/sulfur impurities leading to nonmetallic inclusions.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: The poor ductility of Fe-9Al AIMFC (0.1% elongation) is attributed to microcracks from residual hydrogen, whereas VIM Fe-9Al shows good ductility (9% elongation) and hot workability.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_9.value: Increasing carbon content in Fe-7Al alloy from 0.002 to 1.0 wt.% resulted in significant improvement in room-temperature strength (YS 382–891 MPa, UTS 425–1120 MPa) but adversely affected ductility (EL 21–1.5%) due to increasing volume fraction of Fe3AlC0.5 precipitates.
